definition: The unit LLMs actually read — roughly ¾ of an English word. You pay, and wait, per token.
The model's working memory: system prompt + retrieved documents + your prompt + its answer must all fit inside it.
Retrieval-Augmented Generation — the system searches your documents first and pastes the best matches into the context window before the model answers.
A list of numbers representing meaning; similar texts get nearby numbers, which is how retrieval finds relevant chunks.
Model Context Protocol — an open standard that lets models discover and call external tools (search, tickets, email) with structured arguments.
A confident-sounding fabrication. Risk rises when the prompt is vague and retrieval returns junk.
Hidden instructions the app puts before your prompt — rules, persona, and safety policy.
How many of the highest-similarity document chunks retrieval passes to the model.
Persona, Task, Context, Format — the four load-bearing elements of a strong prompt.
Splitting a big job into a sequence of small prompts, feeding each output into the next.
